title: Deyeuxia zenkeri

This classification is in line with the latest updates in the taxonomy of grasses.

intro: Deyeuxia zenkeri, also known as Zenker's grass, is a type of plant in the grass family (Poaceae) that is native to Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia zenkeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Deyeuxia zenkeri is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
